name: High Volume of File Modification/Deletion in Short Span
description: Detects a high volume (30 or more) of file modifications or deletions within a one-minute timeframe. This can indicate ransomware activity, data wiping, or other malicious behavior.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of the last detected file modification/deletion event in the sequence.

parameters.name: event_id
parameters.description: The Sysmon Event ID for the file operation (11 for modification/creation, 23 for deletion).

parameters.name: process_name
parameters.description: The name of the process performing the high volume of file operations.

parameters.name: process_path
parameters.description: The full path to the process performing the high volume of file operations.

parameters.name: target_filename
parameters.description: The name of a file involved in the high volume of modifications/deletions. (Note: This will likely be one of many, as it's an aggregated alert).

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which the process performed the file operations.

parameters.name: computer_name
parameters.description: The name of the computer where the event occurred.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: The total number of file modifications/deletions detected within the specified interval.
